$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_d: Connect an AI model from Azure OpenAI as a knowledge source.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_a: The API version to use for this operation. This follows the YYYY-MM-DD format.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_d: Deployments provide endpoints to the Azure OpenAI base models, or your fine-tuned models, configured with settings to meet your needs, including the content moderation model, version handling, and deployment size.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_t: Controls randomness. Lowering the temperature means that the model will produce more repetitive and deterministic responses. Increasing the temperature will result in more unexpected or creative responses. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.

Similar to temperature, this controls randomness but uses a different method. Lowering Top P will narrow the model's token selection to likelier tokens. Increasing Top P will let the model choose from tokens with both high and low likelihood. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_s: Make the model end its response at a desired point. The model response will end before the specified sequence, so it won't contain the stop sequence text. For ChatGPT, using |im_end| ensures that the model response doesn't generate a follow-up user query. You can include as many as four stop sequences.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataKnowledgeSource_m: Set a limit on the number of tokens per model response. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length. One token is roughly 4 characters for typical English text. If left empty, the model will use its max context length.

Specify the column in your index that contains the original title of each document.
Specify the column in your index that contains the original URL of each document.
Specify the column in your index that contains the original file name of each document.
Columns in your index that contain the main text content of each document.
Columns in your index that contain vector data.

Specifies the name of the model deployment to use for vectorization. This model deployment must be in the same Azure OpenAI resource specified in the connection, but On Your Data will use this model deployment via an internal call rather than a public one, which enables vector search even in private networks.
Select a semantic search configuration to use with your search index.
Limit responses to your data content
The strictness of the search relevance filtering (range: 1 to 5). Higher strictness results in higher precision but lower recall of the answer.
The number of top documents to feature for a query.

$$IntentBase_availableInChannels_descrip: List of channel IDs that the intent is available in.

$$RecurrenceTrigger_description$$: A trigger that gets invoked based on a recurrence
$$TriggerBase_actions_description$$: The list of actions that should be executed when triggered.
$$TriggerBase_id_description$$: The unique identifier for the trigger within the scope of this topic.
$$TriggerBase_condition_description$$: An optional condition that decides whether the topic should be triggered. Ignored when the topic is redirected to explicitly.
$$TriggerBase_priority_description$$: Set priority for this trigger against other qualifying triggers with lower numbers indicating higher priority.

$$CreateConversationConnectorTrigger_des: A trigger that gets invoked based on an external signal and creates a new conversation.

$$OnRedirect_description$$: Activates only when redirected to, this topic is not triggered automatically.

$$OnGeneratedResponse_description$$: Triggered when a response is generated by the agent, after all topics, actions and knowledge have been called, prior to it being sent to the conversation.

$$OnActivity_description$$: Triggered when an activity is received. Optionally filtered to a specific activity type.

$$OnEventActivity_description$$: Triggered when an activity of type Event is received. Optionally filtered to a specific event name.
$$OnEventActivity_eventName_description$: Optional filter for a specific event name.
$$OnDtmfKeyPress_description$$: Triggered when a DTMF key tone is received. Node-level DTMF configurations overrule bot-level DTMF configurations or triggers.

$$OnInactivity_description$$: Triggered when no user input has been received for a specified amount of time

$$OnSelectIntent_triggerBehavior_descrip: Control when this trigger will execute
$$OnError_description$$: Triggered when an error occurs. The ErrorCode and ErrorMessage variables are available to get more details about the error.

$$OnSignIn_description$$: Triggered when sign in is required.
$$OnUnknownIntent_description$$: Triggered when a user's message matches no intents.

$$OnSilence_description$$: Triggered when a duration has passed without the user providing any speech or DTMF input. Silence detection must be turned on in bot settings.

$$AdaptiveDialog_description$$: A topic has trigger phrases and conversation nodes. Trigger phrases are phrases, keywords, and questions that a user is likely to type that are related to a specific issue. Conversation nodes define how a bot should respond to a trigger phrase and what it should do.

$$AdaptiveDialog_triggers_description$$: A list of triggers that are evaluated when this topic is active or on the stack.
$$AdaptiveDialog_endAutomatically_descri: Defines whether the topic should automatically exit when the end of the dialog is reached. This is enabled by default and should only be disabled when additonal triggers have been defined on this topic.

$$AdaptiveDialog_outputType_description$: Schema for the results of this dialog.

$$ExternalAction_output_description$$: Maps outputs of this action to variables.
$$InvokeFlowAction_flowId_description$$: The unique identifier of the Cloud Flow.

$$InvokeSkillAction_skillId_description$: The unique name (schemaname) of the skill component for which the action is referenced.
$$InvokeSkillAction_actionId_description: The id of the action within the skill to invoke.

$$InvokeSkillAction_group_interruptions_: Decide if the customer can switch to another topic during this skill action.
$$LatencyMessageSettings_allowLatencyMes: If true, action will be allowed to send a latency message to the user.
$$LatencyMessageSettings_latencyMessage_: The activity to send to the user
$$LatencyMessageSettings_latencyMessageD: This setting determines how long the bot will wait before delivering the latency message after a background operation request has started. Timing is set in milliseconds.
$$LatencyMessageSettings_minimumPlayback: The latency message will play for this amount of time, at least, even if the background operation completes while the message is playing. Timing is set in milliseconds.
$$AdaptiveCardPrompt_description$$: Send an adaptive card to the user and save the response in a variable. You can also use this node to validate user input and reprompt if the input doesn't meet certain criteria.
$$InputDialog_unrecognizedPrompt_descrip: Send this message again when a valid response isn't identified.
$$InputDialog_repeatCount_description$$: The maximum number of times this message will be repeated when a valid response isn't identified.
$$AdaptiveCardPrompt_card_description$$: The adaptive card to send to the user
$$AdaptiveCardPrompt_output_description$: Maps outputs of this card to variables.
$$Question_description$$: Ask a question and save the response in a variable. You can also use this node to validate user input and reprompt if the input doesn't meet certain criteria.
$$Question_alwaysPrompt_description$$: If true, the question will be skipped if the variable already has a value.
$$Question_skipQuestionMode_description$: Controls when to ask the question if the variable already has a value.
$$Question_variable_description$$: The variable into which the response will be saved
$$Question_prompt_description$$: The activity to send to the user
$$Question_invalidPrompt_description$$: The prompt to send when the identified entity doesn't pass the validation rules.
$$Question_defaultValueResponse_descript: The message to send when the maximum retry count has been reached and the default entity is used.
$$Question_validation_description$$: Additional criteria the entity must meet to be considered valid. Must be a formula that evaluates as true or false (e.g. 'Topic.Var1 < 10').
$$Question_defaultValue_description$$: The value to assign the result variable when the maximum retry count has been reached and no entity has been identified.
$$Question_holdSettings_description$$: When you turn this on, the agent will pause and wait for a resume word to restart the conversation
$$Question_tile_questionBehavior_descrip: Control how and when your bot asks this question
$$Question_group_skipBehavior_descriptio: Decide if the question should be skipped if the variable already has a value
$$Question_group_reprompt_description$$: If the bot doesn't get a valid answer to the question, it can ask the question again
$$Question_group_advancedMessageSettings: Configure additional settings on the message that will be sent
$$Question_tile_entityRecognition_descri: Additional properties for the entity type being recognized
$$Question_group_additionalEntityValidat: Configure additional validation behavior beyond the default for the entity type
$$Question_group_noValidEntityFound_desc: Choose what happens if the bot didn't identify an entity, even after repeating the question
$$Question_tile_interruptions_descriptio: Configure when this action can be interrupted
$$Question_group_interruptionSettings_de: Decide if the customer can switch to another topic during this action
$$Question_group_bargeIn_description$$: Users can interrupt bot during audio play back
$$Question_group_holdSettings_descriptio: Your settings for pausing and resuming conversations between your agent and users.
$$VoiceInputSettings_wordsetsWeightValue: The weight of the wordsets, value between 0.0 and 1.0.
$$VoiceInputSettings_speechRecognitionSe: Controls the level of sensitivity to speech, value between 0.0 and 1.0.
$$VoiceInputSettings_speechRecognitionSp: Specifies the desired balance between speed and accuracy.
$$VoiceInputSettings_silenceDetectionTim: The timeout limit applies when the agent doesn't detect any user input. If no user input is detected within the time limit, the agent can re-engage with the user. Silence detection for voice speech begins timing after the user finishes speaking.
$$VoiceInputSettings_repeatCountOnSilenc: If the bot continues detects silence, it can ask the question again or send a new message.
$$VoiceInputSettings_inputTimeoutRespons: Send this message again when a silence detection no input event is recevied.
$$VoiceInputSettings_defaultValueMissing: Action to do when exceeding re-prompt attempt or no re-prompt is configured.
$$VoiceInputSettings_utteranceEndTimeout: This timeout limit applies when the user pauses during or after speech. If the pause is longer than the timeout limit, the bot presumes the user has finished speaking. The default value for the utterance end timeout is 1500 milliseconds.
$$VoiceInputSettings_speechRecognitionTi: This timeout limit determines how much time the bot allows for the user's input once they begin speaking. The default value for the speech recognition timeout is 12000 milliseconds.
$$VoiceInputSettings_defaultValueOnSilen: Action to do when exceeding re-prompt attempt or no re-prompt is configured.
$$VoiceInputSettings_group_silenceDetect: Choose how your bot responds if it doesn't detect any input from your end user
$$AutomaticHoldSettings_resumeWords_desc: A list of common words a user can say to resume a conversation.
$$AutomaticHoldSettings_holdMusic_descri: Enter a URL to a .wav audio file that plays when the converstaion is on hold.
$$AutomaticHoldSettings_holdTimeoutInMil: Decide how long the agent should wait before prompting the user to resume the conversation.
$$AutomaticHoldSettings_holdRepeatCount_: Decide how many times the agent will prompt the user to resume the conversation.
$$AutomaticHoldSettings_holdTimeoutMessa: The message the agents sends when prompting the user to resume the conversation. It includes an example of the resume words you chose.

$$CSATQuestion_description$$: Asks the user for a rating between 1 and 5. CSAT stands for customer satisfaction score
$$CSATQuestion_variable_description$$: Store the response rating (1-5) in this variable.
$$OAuthInput_description$$: Prompts the user to sign in.
$$OAuthInput_title_description$$: The title of the sign-in card
$$OAuthInput_text_description$$: Any additional text to include in the sign-in card
$$OAuthInput_resource_description$$: Optional target to direct what resource to authenticate to
$$ActionScope_title$$: A sequence of actions to execute. Action scopes should not be added to a topic explicitly, it is used as part of conditions and loops.
$$ActionScope_actionScope_DisplayName$$: A sequence of actions to execute. Action scopes should not be added to a topic explicitly, it is used as part of conditions and loops.
$$Foreach_description$$: Executes a set of actions for each item in the provided list.
$$Foreach_value_description$$: The variable to store the item of the loop in.
$$Foreach_index_description$$: The optional variable to store the zero-based index of the items in.
$$BaseInvokeDialogOrSelf_activityProcess: When set to false, reprocesses the message after redirecting to the target dialog.
$$RepeatDialog_description$$: Repeats the current dialog from the start, clearing all existing variables.
$$ActivateExternalTrigger_description$$: Enable an external trigger for the user. Will do not do anything if the trigger is already enabled.
$$ActivateExternalTrigger_externalTrigge: Save new external trigger identifier as
The variable in which to store the new external trigger identifier.
$$DisableTrigger_description$$: Action which un-registers an external trigger based topic
$$CancelAllDialogsBase_activityProcessed: When set to false, reprocesses the message after redirecting to the target dialog.
$$BreakLoop_description$$: Used inside loops, such as for each item, to immediately exit the loop.
$$ContinueLoop_description$$: Used inside loops, such as for each item, to skip to the next item.
$$ResetVariable_description$$: Resets the value of a variable, potentially causing re-evaluation of the default value, question or action that provides the value to this variable.
$$EditTableV2_description$$: Add, remove or take items from a list variable. Depending on the change type, this action can add an item to the end of the list, take an item from the beginning or the end of the list, remove an item from anywhere in the list, or clear all items from the list.
$$AddItemOperation_description$$: Add an item to the list
$$RemoveItemOperation_description$$: Remove an item from the list
$$TakeFirstItemOperation_description$$: Take the last item from the beginning of the list
$$TakeLastItemOperation_description$$: Take the last item in the list
$$ClearItemsOperation_description$$: Remove all items from the list
$$EndDialog_description$$: Ends the current dialog topic and returns control to the caller.
$$EndDialog_clearTopicQueue_description$: Clears upcoming topics that were queued for the current activity.
$$HttpRequestAction_description$$: Choose a REST API to invoke/sends an HTTP request to an external endpoint.
$$HttpRequestAction_body_description$$: Decide what to send or send nothing

$$HttpRequestAction_response_description: The variable to store the result in.
$$HttpRequestAction_responseSchema_descr: The schema of the HTTP response object.
$$HttpRequestAction_responseHeaders_desc: Variable to store response headers in
$$RawRequestContent_content_description$: The raw content to be added into the HTTP request
$$ContinueOnErrorBehavior_errorResponseB: Variable of the error response body
$$EndConversation_description$$: End the conversation when the user is done interacting with the bot or when the bot can't help the user any further. Channel-specific behavior applies when conversations end. For telephony, the call will hang up.
$$RecognizeIntent_description$$: Identifies the intent of the provided input and starts a new conversational flow
$$TransferConversation_description$$: Transfers the conversation to an external system.
$$TransferConversation_messageToAgent_de: Contents of the message to send to the agent as part of the transfer.
$$TransferConversation_context_descripti: Specify context data to include in the transfer.
$$TransferConversation_telephoneNumber_d: When using telephony, specify the telephone number to which the conversation will be transfered.
$$TransferConversationV2_description$$: Transfers the conversation to an external system.
$$TransferToAgent_messageToAgent_descrip: Contents of the message to send to the agent as part of the transfer.
$$TransferToAgent_context_description$$: Specify context data to include in the transfer.
$$TransferToPhoneNumber_sipUuiHeader_des: The Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) header carries user-to-user call control information between systems. In IVR, the SIP UUI header can be used to pass contextual information like a customer ID or an account number to an external system. If the header exceeds 128 characters, it will be truncated for your agent. Use the test chat to confirm your SIP UUI header will appear appropriately.
$$TransferToPhoneNumber_sipxHeaders_desc: SIP X-headers to send with this transfer
$$TransferToPhoneNumber_sipxHeaders_Plac: Enter SIP X-headers Key and Value
$$TransferToPhoneNumber_dtmfSequence_des: DTMF sequence to send during the External Phone Transfer
$$TransferToPhoneNumber_group_externalCa: When making an external call transfer, you can include context when call transfer takes place over SIP.
$$ExpressionTransferContext_value_descri: Values to be included when sending the handoff activity
$$SetVariable_description$$: Sets a variable to a provided value
$$SetTextVariable_description$$: Sets a variable to a value that is translated when the bot's message content is translated.
$$SetTextVariable_value_description$$: The text value to set the variable to.
$$ParseValue_description$$: Parses a string or untyped value to the provided data type. When the input is a string, it will be treated as JSON.
$$ParseValue_errorPolicy_description$$: Policy for handling properties in the data at execution time which were not defined in the original structure.
$$SignOutUser_description$$: Signs out the currently signed in user
$$QuickReplyBase_title_description$$: The text to display on the button. If not specified, a default value will be shown depending on the type of quick reply.
$$QuickReplyBase_image_description$$: An image URL or base64-encoded image string to display next to the button text.
$$QuickReplyBase_imageAltText_descriptio: The alternative text to display on the image.
$$SendMessageQuickReply_text_description: The text to send to the bot when clicked.
$$SendMessageQuickReply_displayText_desc: The text to display as a sent message in the chat history after the user has clicked.
$$OpenUrlQuickReply_url_description$$: The full URL to open when clicked.
$$MakeCallQuickReply_description$$: Sends a link that starts a call.
$$MakeCallQuickReply_phoneNumber_descrip: The phone number to call when clicked.
$$SendHiddenMessageQuickReply_descriptio: Sends a plain text message not shown in chat history.
$$SendHiddenMessageQuickReply_text_descr: The text to send to the bot when clicked. This will not be shown in the chat history after sending.
$$InboundTranscriptRedactionSettings_isR: Redact credit card numbers from inbound call and chat transcripts and logs. Audio recordings aren't redacted, and outbound interactions aren't included.
Redact Social Security numbers from inbound call and chat transcripts and logs. Audio recordings aren't redacted, and outbound interactions aren't included.
$$BotConfiguration_isTelephonyEnabled_de: Set up your bot for voice-first channels like telephony.
$$BotConfiguration_sensitiveDataMaskingS: Redact sensitive data, such as credit card or social security numbers, within logging transcripts.
$$BotConfiguration_isAgentConnectable_ti: Let other agents connect to and use this one
$$BotConfiguration_isAgentConnectable_de: Let agents work together to complete workflows
$$BotConfiguration_voiceProcessingMode_d: Represents the mode of voice used by the agent, classic or realtime.
$$GPTSettings_defaultSchemaName_descript: Schema name of the GPT to use as default
$$AISettings_isFileAnalysisEnabled_descr: Apply file analysis capability when files are uploaded to a chat
$$AISettings_isSemanticSearchEnabled_des: Allows semantic search with Sharepoint and Graph connectors
$$ContentModerationSettings_moderatedCus: When potential responses get flagged by content moderation, send:
I can not help with that. Is there something else I can help with?
$$CurrentModels_connection_description$$: An optional connection to use for this model
$$ToolRoiCalculationConfiguration_schema: The schema name of the tool
$$ToolRoiCalculationConfiguration_savedC: The cost value it will save
$$ToolRoiCalculationConfiguration_savedT: The time in seconds it will save
$$ToolBasedRoiCalculationSettings_tools_: List of tool configurations for ROI
$$SessionBasedRoiCalculationSettings_sav: The cost value it will save
The time in seconds it will save
$$AnalyticsSettings_includeNodeLevelDeta: Include node level details in the transcript
$$AnalyticsSettings_roiCalculation_descr: Return on investment calculation settings for analytics
$$IvrBotSettings_domainLanguageModelWeig: The weight of the domain-specific language model compared to the base, value between 0 and 1. This can be set per local.
$$IvrBotSettings_interruptionEagerness_d: This property is a way to control how eager the model is to interrupt the user.
$$BotSpeechSettings_interdigitTimeoutInM: This timeout limit applies when your user hasn't met the minimum input length. It applies only to multi-digit DTMF input. The interdigit timeout must be a positive whole number and no special characters or letters can be used. The minimum recommended limit length is 500 ms.
$$BotSpeechSettings_terminationTimeoutIn: This timeout limit applies when the user has met the maximum input length. It applies only to multi-digit DTMF input. After the limit times out, the bot will accept the input as complete and won't wait for more input. If the termination timeout is turned off, input will immediately be considered complete after it matches the maximum length specification. The termination timeout must be a positive whole number and no special characters or letters can be used.
$$BotSpeechSettings_isDtmfSettingsEnable: Enable DTMF settings for voice agent.
$$BotSpeechSettings_silenceDetectionTime: The timeout limit applies when the bot doesn't detect any user input. If no user input is detected within the time limit, the bot can re-engage with the user. Silence detection for telephony begins timing after the IVR finishes speaking.
$$BotSpeechSettings_utteranceEndTimeoutI: This timeout limit applies when the user pauses during or after speech. If the pause is longer than the timeout limit, the bot presumes the user has finished speaking. The default value for the utterance end timeout is 1500 milliseconds.
$$BotSpeechSettings_speechRecognitionTim: This timeout limit determines how much time the bot allows for the user's input once they begin speaking. The default value for the speech recognition timeout is 12000 milliseconds.
$$BotSpeechSettings_latencyMessage_descr: The activity to send to the user
$$BotSpeechSettings_latencyMessageDelayI: This setting determines how long the bot will wait before delivering the latency message after a background operation request has started. Timing is set in milliseconds.
$$BotSpeechSettings_minimumPlaybackInMil: The latency message will play for this amount of time, at least, even if the background operation completes while the message is playing. Timing is set in milliseconds.
$$BotSpeechSettings_isDomainLanguageMode: When enabled, a domain-specific language model will be trained on trigger phrases and entity samples in the bot when it is published.
$$BotSpeechSettings_domainLanguageModelW: The weight of the domain-specific language model compared to the base, value between 0 and 1.
$$BotSpeechSettings_wordsetsWeightValue_: The weight of the wordsets, value between 0.0 and 1.0.
$$BotSpeechSettings_speechRecognitionSen: Controls the level of sensitivity to speech, value between 0.0 and 1.0.
$$BotSpeechSettings_speechRecognitionSpe: Specifies the desired balance between speed and accuracy.
$$BotSpeechSettings_autoAddLanguageTagIn: When enabled, automatically add language tag around the voice prompt message. This allows for precise language control and improves accuracy when handling multilingual content.
$$BotSpeechSettings_prefixPaddingInMilli: The amount of audio the agent processes before the user begins speaking.
$$BotSpeechSettings_audioThreshold_descr: Controls the level of audio threshold value between 0.0 and 1.0.
$$BotSpeechSettings_speechEndTimeoutInMi: The amount of time the agent waits before it decides the user has finished speaking.
$$TeamsApplicationDetails_supportsCallin: This allows MSTeams voice call to MCS agent where MCS provisions ACS and event grid resources
$$TeamsApplicationDetails_isTeamsIvrEnab: This allows MSTeams Auto-Attendant to call MCS agent where MSTeams itself configures the ACS provisioning and connects to MCS via conversation conductor channel
$$DtmfMultipleChoiceOptions_generateMapp: Enable assigning DTMF keys to options
Only supported for Boolean, List variables, Multiple choice options, and Custom close list entities.
$$DtmfMultipleChoiceOptions_readOutOptio: Only supported for Boolean, list variables, multiple choice options, and closed list entities.
$$EntityReferenceWithMetadata_includeMet: Determines whether additional metadata should be returned with the entity recognition results.
$$DynamicClosedListEntityReference_sampl: Advanced Natural Language Understanding training samples to enhance entity recognition
$$ClosedListEntity_optionSetName_descrip: Name of the option set in Power FX used to reference items in the list.
$$ClosedListEntity_group_entitySettings_: Configure additional settings for your selected entity type

$$CreateSearchQuery_result_description$$: Store the produced output in this variable

$$SearchAndSummarizeWithCustomModel_know: Get search results from the knowledge sources using selected mode.
$$SearchAndSummarizeContent_description$: Search for content or provide your own context, use OpenAI to create a summarized response using the provided data.

$$SearchAllFiles_description$$: Get search results using all of the uploaded files.

$$ExternalModelConfiguration_temperature: Controls randomness. Lowering the temperature means that the model will produce more repetitive and deterministic responses. Increasing the temperature will result in more unexpected or creative responses. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.
$$ExternalModelConfiguration_topP_descri: Similar to temperature, this controls randomness but uses a different method. Lowering Top P will narrow the model's token selection to likelier tokens. Increasing Top P will let the model choose from tokens with both high and low likelihood. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.
$$ExternalModelConfiguration_stopSequenc: Make the model end its response at a desired point. The model response will end before the specified sequence, so it won't contain the stop sequence text. For ChatGPT, using |im_end| ensures that the model response doesn't generate a follow-up user query. You can include as many as four stop sequences.
$$ExternalModelConfiguration_maxTokens_d: Set a limit on the number of tokens per model response. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length. One token is roughly 4 characters for typical English text. If left empty, the model will use its max context length.
$$ExternalModelConfiguration_additionalP: Additional model specific properties. Use this property when you want to pass additional parameters that you know the underlying model can support.
$$ExternalModelConfiguration_deployment_: The name of the model deployment
$$ExternalChatCompletionModelConfigurati: Conversation history type to include when preprocessing the text.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_descriptio: Connect an AI model from Azure OpenAI as a data source.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_apiVersion: The API version to use for this operation. This follows the YYYY-MM-DD format.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_deployment: Deployments provide endpoints to the Azure OpenAI base models, or your fine-tuned models, configured with settings to meet your needs, including the content moderation model, version handling, and deployment size.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_temperatur: Controls randomness. Lowering the temperature means that the model will produce more repetitive and deterministic responses. Increasing the temperature will result in more unexpected or creative responses. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_topP_descr: Similar to temperature, this controls randomness but uses a different method. Lowering Top P will narrow the model's token selection to likelier tokens. Increasing Top P will let the model choose from tokens with both high and low likelihood. Try adjusting temperature or Top P but not both.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_stopSequen: Make the model end its response at a desired point. The model response will end before the specified sequence, so it won't contain the stop sequence text. For ChatGPT, using |im_end| ensures that the model response doesn't generate a follow-up user query. You can include as many as four stop sequences.
$$AzureOpenAIOnYourDataSource_maxTokens_: Set a limit on the number of tokens per model response. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length. One token is roughly 4 characters for typical English text. If left empty, the model will use its max context length.
$$CustomSearchInputContent_description$$: A single search result provided manually to the system.
$$CustomSearchInputContent_content_descr: Search result content to be used by the model for summarization.
$$SearchResultRecord_content_description: Search result content to be used by the model for summarization.
$$SearchResultRecord_knowledgeName_descr: The given name of the knowledge source
$$AnswerQuestionWithAI_description$$: Uses AI to continue a conversation. Includes recent interactions between the user and the bot.

$$TaskDialog_modelDisplayName_descriptio: Overrides the display name of this component when used by AI models.
$$TaskDialog_modelDescription_descriptio: Overrides the description of this component when used by AI models.
$$TaskDialog_outputs_description$$: Override descriptions for outputs of this task.
$$TaskDialog_triggerCondition_title$$: When this tool may be used
$$TaskDialog_triggerCondition_descriptio: Indicates whether the tool may be used by the agent. When true, the tool may be called. When false, the tool may not be used unless called directly by a topic or agent.
$$TaskDialog_triggerCondition_option_tru: Agent may use this tool at any time
$$TaskDialog_triggerCondition_option_fal: Only when referenced by topics or agents
$$QuestionInputSettings_prompt_descripti: The activity to send to the user. Can be either a custom message or AI-generated. If not provided, it will default to AI-generated.
$$QuestionInputSettings_repeatCount_desc: The maximum number of times this message will be repeated when a valid response isn't identified.
$$QuestionInputSettings_validation_descr: Additional criteria the entity must meet to be considered valid. Must be a formula that evaluates as true or false (e.g. 'Topic.Var1 < 10').
$$QuestionInputSettings_defaultValue_des: The value to assign the result variable when the maximum retry count has been reached and no entity has been identified.
$$QuestionInputSettings_invalidPrompt_de: The prompt to send when the identified entity doesn't pass the validation rules.
$$QuestionInputSettings_defaultValueResp: The message to send when the maximum retry count has been reached and the default entity is used.
$$QuestionInputSettings_unrecognizedProm: Send this message again when a valid response isn't identified.
$$QuestionInputSettings_group_copilotPro: Configure how the agent will ask the user to fill the input
$$QuestionInputSettings_group_copilotDoe: If the agent doesn't identify an entity in the user's statement, it can ask the question again
$$QuestionInputSettings_group_additional: Configure additional validation behavior beyond the default for the entity type
$$QuestionInputSettings_group_noValidEnt: Choose what happens if the agent didn't identify an entity, even after repeating the question
$$AutomaticTaskInput_description$$: Let AI determine how this input should be collected. Optionally provide additional display and description information to the guide the model.
$$TaskInput_propertyName_description$$: Name of the input property to bind to.
$$AutomaticTaskInput_name_description$$: Name of the task input. By default will come from the task action input but may be overwritten.
$$AutomaticTaskInput_description_descrip: Description of the task input. By default will come from the task action input but may be overwritten.
$$AutomaticTaskInput_shouldPromptUser_de: When false, this input will not be prompted by LLM to the user, but LLM will still slot fill this input if it recognizes an answer from the user prompt.
$$AutomaticTaskInput_defaultValue_descri: The default value to use instead of prompting the user
$$ManualTaskInput_description$$: Provide an exact value for this input. This input will not be considered by AI.
$$ManualTaskInput_value_description$$: The value to bind to the input.
$$TaskOutput_propertyName_description$$: Name of the output property to bind to.
$$TaskOutput_name_description$$: Name of the task output. By default will come from the task action output but may be overwritten.
$$TaskOutput_description_description$$: Description of the task output. By default will come from the task action output but may be overwritten.
$$InvokeClientTaskAction_name_descriptio: The name of the function to be invoked.
$$InvokeClientTaskAction_description_des: A description of what the function does.
$$InvokeClientTaskAction_clientActionInp: The inputs sent to the client in an event
$$InvokeClientTaskAction_clientActionRes: The outputs returned from the client

$$AlwaysRequireMCPApproval_tools_descrip: List of MCP tools that always require approval. If empty, all tools will require approval.

$$LatencySettingsBaseLegacy_minimumPlayb: The latency message will play for this amount of time, at least, even if the background operation completes while the message is playing. Timing is set in milliseconds.
$$RealtimeVoiceMessageLatencySettings_la: The latency message to play for c2 users
$$RealtimeVoiceAudioLatencySettings_desc: Settings for playing audio during latency
$$RealtimeVoiceAudioLatencySettings_late: The latency audio to play for c2 users
$$RealtimeVoiceAudioLatencySettings_fall: The fallback latency message to play for c2 users
$$ActivityTemplateBase_channelData_descr: Contains parameters specific to the channel to be handled by the client.
$$ActivityTemplateBase_value_description: Contains parameters specific to the activity to be handled by the client.
$$EventActivityTemplate_name_description: Indicates the purpose of the event to be handled by the client.
$$MessageActivityTemplate_allowBargeIn_d: Users can interrupt bot during audio play back.
$$MessageActivityTemplate_randomizeVaria: When enabled, reprompts are played in random order. When disabled, they are played sequentially.
$$Citation_text_description$$: Text content of the citation, this is typically the entire chunk obtained from search results.
$$Citation_url_description$$: Full URL linking to the source page or file.
$$Citation_iconUrl_description$$: Icon URL associated with file or page location.
$$DtmfTerminationOptions_interdigitTimeo: This timeout limit applies when your user hasn't met the minimum input length. It applies only to multi-digit DTMF input. The interdigit timeout must be a positive whole number and no special characters or letters can be used. The minimum recommended limit length is 500 ms.
$$DtmfTerminationOptions_terminationTime: This timeout limit applies when the user has met the maximum input length. It applies only to multi-digit DTMF input. After the limit times out, the bot will accept the input as complete and won't wait for more input. If the termination timeout is turned off, input will immediately be considered complete after it matches the maximum length specification. The termination timeout must be a positive whole number and no special characters or letters can be used.
$$FixedLengthDtmfTerminationOptions_grou: Configure how your bot receives input from a telephone dial pad
$$FlexibleLengthDtmfTerminationOptions_g: Configure how your bot receives input from a telephone dial pad
$$KeyBasedDtmfTerminationOptions_group_d: Configure how your bot receives input from a telephone dial pad
$$StringPrebuiltEntity_speechRecognition: Numbers up to nine are spelled out ("one"), while higher numbers are in numerals ("10").
$$StringPrebuiltEntity_group_responseFor: Choose what your user will see.
$$DatePrebuiltEntity_ambiguousResolution: Controls how ambiguous date/time values are resolved
$$DateTimePrebuiltEntity_ambiguousResolu: Controls how ambiguous date/time values are resolved
$$DateTimeNoTimeZonePrebuiltEntity_ambig: Controls how ambiguous date/time values are resolved
$$FilePrebuiltEntity_includeFileMetadata: Determine whether the file is represented as a record with Content, Name and ContentType, or just a File variable
$$TestCaseDefinition_index_description$$: The position of a Test Case in a Test Set.
$$TestTranscriptDefinition_description$$: Contains the activities of the TestCase and its information
$$TestActivityDefinition_originType_desc: Type of Origin of a Test Activity.
$$IntentMatchAssertion_description$$: Evaluates whether the user's input aligns with a predefined intent category.
$$CosineEmbeddingSimilarityAssertion_des: Cosine Embedding Similarity measures how similar two vectors are by calculating the cosine of the angle between them. If the vectors point in the same direction,                 the similarity is close to 1; if they are orthogonal or opposite, the similarity is close to 0 or -1.
$$EvaluationSet_description$$: A set of data for evaluation along with graders that evaluate outputs.
$$EvaluationSet_graders_description$$: The graders that will be used to evaluate the agent and model outputs.
$$EvaluationData_description$$: A set of data for evaluation along with expected responses
$$ToolCallGrader_description$$: Validates that specific tools or topics were invoked during execution.
$$ExpectedExecutionStepBase_schemaName_d: The schema name of the expected component to be invoked.
$$CodeGrader_name_description$$: The display name for this grader.
$$CodeGrader_code_description$$: Python code that evaluates the agent's response. Code must define a grade(conversation, input) function that returns a float score that will be compared against the threshold.
$$CodeGrader_threshold_description$$: The passing score threshold (0.0 to 1.0). Test cases pass when the grade function returns a score larger or equal to this value.
$$enum_TopicStartBehavior_Default_descri: No special action taken when the topic starts.
$$enum_TopicStartBehavior_CancelOtherTop: Cancel all other topics before starting this topic.
$$enum_TopicStartBehavior_UseLatestPubli: Use latest published content and cancel other topics.
Cancel all other topics before starting this topic. Look for the lastest published version, and apply it to this conversation.
$$enum_OnSelectIntentTriggerBehavior_Def: Does not trigger when a question is interrupted.
$$enum_OnSelectIntentTriggerBehavior_Alw: Trigger either when multiple topics are matched, or when multiple potential topics are found when a question is interrupted.
$$enum_ConversationOutcome_ResolvedConfi: If the dialog action is reached then the current session is considered resolved for the purpose of analytics. The session ends immediately for the purpose of analytics.
$$enum_ConversationOutcome_ResolvedImpli: If the dialog action is reached and user didn't reply then we consider that the current session is in assumed resolved for the purpose of analytics.
$$enum_ConversationOutcome_Escalated_des: If the dialog action is reached then the current session is considered escalated for the purpose of analytics.
$$enum_VariablesToClear_ConversationScop: Global variables for the current session
$$enum_VariablesToClear_ConversationHist: Conversation history for the current session
$$enum_NetworkIsolationType_PublicNetwor: Use public addresses and internet for network communication.
$$enum_NetworkIsolationType_VirtualNetwo: Use virtual network subnet delegated to the Power Platform.
$$enum_ProvisioningState_Provisioned_des: The bot is fully provisioned including BCR resources.
$$enum_ProvisioningState_ProvisionedWith: The bot is provisioned but it doesn't have app and bot registration in azure.
$$enum_ProvisioningState_ContentReady_de: The bot is content is ready for use. The bot is still provisioning.
$$enum_ContentModerationLevel_Minimum_de: Lowest moderation, allowing the bot to generate many responses, though they may include inaccuracies
$$enum_ContentModerationLevel_Low_descri: Bot generates most responses, but responses can have inaccuracies
$$enum_ContentModerationLevel_Medium_des: Bot generates more responses, but responses may be less relevant
$$enum_ContentModerationLevel_High_descr: Bot generates fewer responses, but responses are relevant
$$enum_ContentModerationLevel_Maximum_de: Highest moderation, limiting the bot's responses to ensure only the most accurate and relevant content
$$enum_ToneType_Conversational_descripti: Clear, casual, easy to understand, and familiar
$$enum_SharePointIndexBehavior_Disabled_: Do not use any advanced indexing for SharePoint queries.
$$enum_SharePointIndexBehavior_EnabledWi: Use advanced indexing for SharePoint queries. If a user does not have access, fallback to a non-indexed query.
Use advanced indexing for SharePoint queries. If a user does not have access, return no results.
$$enum_TaskActionOutputMode_All_descript: All outputs from the action definition will be included
$$enum_TaskActionOutputMode_Specific_des: User will specify which outputs should be included
$$enum_TurnDetectionType_SilenceBasedVoi: Detect user has stopped speaking based on sound.
$$enum_TurnDetectionType_SemanticVoiceAc: Detect user has stopped speaking based on meaning.
$$enum_AmbiguousDateTimeResolutionBehavi: When the recognized date/time is ambiguous, prompt the user to clarify (default behavior).
When the recognized date/time is ambiguous, automatically pick the next matching occurrence relative to now.
When the recognized date/time is ambiguous, automatically pick the previous matching occurrence relative to now.
$$enum_ParseValueErrorPolicy_ThrowError_: Runtime data that can't be parsed into the appropiate type will raise a Runtime Error
$$enum_ParseValueErrorPolicy_DefaultBlan: Runtime data that can't be parsed into the appropiate type will be ignored and properties without values will be filled with null
$$enum_GenerativeResponseOutputType_Text: Store the textual output in the target variable.
$$enum_GenerativeResponseOutputType_Full: Store the text output, the speech output and the citations in the response variable.
$$enum_BooleanConditionOperator_GreaterE: is greater than or equal to
$$enum_BooleanConditionOperator_LessEqua: is less than or equal to
$$enum_VariableAIVisibility_Hidden_title: The variable can not be used in AI processing
$$enum_VariableAIVisibility_UseInAIConte: The variable can be used in AI processing
$$enum_EnvironmentVariableSecretReadStat: Power virtual agents can read the secret successfully
The bot was not allowlisted to read the secret
'Power Virtual Agents Service' application doesn't have access to the Key Vault
KeyVault or secret was not found
$$enum_WasmInvalidStateMessage_SchemaIsN: SchemaName of the component to validate is null
$$enum_WasmInvalidStateMessage_Component: Component for validation cannot be found
$$enum_WasmInvalidStateMessage_ErrorGett: Error getting dynamic plan tool definition
$$enum_WasmInvalidStateMessage_Translati: Translations have been applied by assigning CurrentLanguage. Changes to the content are not supported
